summary: Reference matrix for positional, named, default, varargs, and overload-call contracts.

cases.upstream_intent: Named arguments and defaults should compose while preserving parameter identity.
cases.zr_contract: Named arguments may reorder explicitly named parameters while omitted parameters consume defaults.
cases.expected_outcome: named reordering with defaults executes and returns the expected value
assertions: argument binding table maps each name once
compiled call metadata records defaults and names
runtime result matches the expected sum

return: named reordering with defaults executes and returns the expected value

parity.skip_reason: binary parity is covered by project/runtime suites; this reference case keeps per-case executable parity optional until manifest-driven parity is wired

binary.skip_reason: project/runtime suites already prove binary execution; this reference case keeps per-case executable parity disabled until the manifest runner can require executed_via=binary

cases.upstream_intent: Mixed positional and named calls need a positive baseline before negative diagnostics are tightened.
cases.zr_contract: Positional arguments may precede named arguments when each parameter is bound once and in a supported order.
cases.expected_outcome: mixed positional and named arguments compile and execute
assertions: parameter order is preserved in call metadata
compiler reports no duplicate binding
runtime result matches expected mapping

return: mixed positional and named arguments compile and execute
cleanup_trace: parameter order is preserved in call metadata

parity.skip_reason: binary parity is covered by project/runtime suites; this reference case keeps per-case executable parity optional until manifest-driven parity is wired

binary.skip_reason: project/runtime suites already prove binary execution; this reference case keeps per-case executable parity disabled until the manifest runner can require executed_via=binary

cases.upstream_intent: A parameter name may not be bound twice across positional and named forms.
cases.zr_contract: Duplicate parameter binding is a compiler error even when the second binding uses a named argument.
assertions: diagnostic names the duplicate parameter
call metadata is not emitted
artifact text omits a callable body for the failing test

parity.skip_reason: binary parity is covered by project/runtime suites; this reference case keeps per-case executable parity optional until manifest-driven parity is wired

binary.skip_reason: project/runtime suites already prove binary execution; this reference case keeps per-case executable parity disabled until the manifest runner can require executed_via=binary

cases.upstream_intent: Unknown keyword arguments should fail as call-shape errors, not as runtime misses.
cases.zr_contract: Unexpected named arguments are rejected during compilation or call-shape checking.
assertions: diagnostic lists the unexpected name
compiler does not silently spill the name into varargs
artifact does not contain a runtime name-fallback branch

parity.skip_reason: binary parity is covered by project/runtime suites; this reference case keeps per-case executable parity optional until manifest-driven parity is wired

binary.skip_reason: project/runtime suites already prove binary execution; this reference case keeps per-case executable parity disabled until the manifest runner can require executed_via=binary

cases.upstream_intent: Varargs should cover zero, one, and many tail arguments consistently.
cases.zr_contract: Vararg collection has explicit minimum and maximum fixed-parameter boundaries before the variadic tail begins.
cases.expected_outcome: zero-tail and many-tail vararg calls keep a stable argument layout
assertions: fixed arity parameters stay distinct from the vararg slice
runtime vararg length is asserted
type inference preserves the tail element type

return: zero-tail and many-tail vararg calls keep a stable argument layout

parity.skip_reason: binary parity is covered by project/runtime suites; this reference case keeps per-case executable parity optional until manifest-driven parity is wired

binary.skip_reason: project/runtime suites already prove binary execution; this reference case keeps per-case executable parity disabled until the manifest runner can require executed_via=binary

cases.upstream_intent: Default handling needs explicit coverage at the boundary between omitted and provided arguments.
cases.zr_contract: Defaults apply only to omitted parameters and never overwrite an explicitly supplied value.
cases.expected_outcome: call sites at the default boundary preserve supplied values and fill omitted slots only
assertions: default slots are recorded explicitly
runtime receives supplied arguments unchanged
artifact text distinguishes omitted and explicit operands

return: call sites at the default boundary preserve supplied values and fill omitted slots only

parity.skip_reason: binary parity is covered by project/runtime suites; this reference case keeps per-case executable parity optional until manifest-driven parity is wired

binary.skip_reason: project/runtime suites already prove binary execution; this reference case keeps per-case executable parity disabled until the manifest runner can require executed_via=binary

cases.upstream_intent: Real call sites combine positional binding, defaults, and later named overrides.
cases.zr_contract: Mixed calls compose positional prefix binding with default fill-in and explicit named overrides without duplicate binding.
cases.expected_outcome: mixed call surfaces compile and run with stable parameter mapping
assertions: parameter binding record shows one source per parameter
module fixture can call the same function across files
runtime result matches expected binding

return: mixed call surfaces compile and run with stable parameter mapping

parity.skip_reason: binary parity is covered by project/runtime suites; this reference case keeps per-case executable parity optional until manifest-driven parity is wired

binary.skip_reason: project/runtime suites already prove binary execution; this reference case keeps per-case executable parity disabled until the manifest runner can require executed_via=binary

cases.upstream_intent: Overload resolution interacting with defaults and varargs needs distinct coverage.
cases.zr_contract: Overload selection resolves against the explicit call shape before any dynamic fallback is considered.
cases.expected_outcome: selected overload remains stable when defaults and varargs are both available
assertions: chosen overload signature is recorded
artifact shows one concrete call target
runtime result proves the chosen overload

return: selected overload remains stable when defaults and varargs are both available

binary.skip_reason: project/runtime suites already prove binary execution; this reference case keeps per-case executable parity disabled until the manifest runner can require executed_via=binary

cases.upstream_intent: Positional arguments after named arguments are a classic stable diagnostic boundary.
cases.zr_contract: Once named binding begins, later positional arguments are rejected by call-shape checking.
assertions: diagnostic family stays stable
argument ordering is printed or recoverable
no runtime fallback path is emitted

cleanup_trace: argument ordering is printed or recoverable

parity.skip_reason: binary parity is covered by project/runtime suites; this reference case keeps per-case executable parity optional until manifest-driven parity is wired

binary.skip_reason: project/runtime suites already prove binary execution; this reference case keeps per-case executable parity disabled until the manifest runner can require executed_via=binary

cases.upstream_intent: Ambiguous call shapes should fail deterministically rather than depending on registration order.
cases.zr_contract: Ambiguous overload resolution is a deterministic compiler error with no load-order dependence.
assertions: diagnostic category is stable
project compile path reproduces the same ambiguity
artifact omits a selected target

parity.skip_reason: binary parity is covered by project/runtime suites; this reference case keeps per-case executable parity optional until manifest-driven parity is wired

binary.skip_reason: project/runtime suites already prove binary execution; this reference case keeps per-case executable parity disabled until the manifest runner can require executed_via=binary

cases.upstream_intent: Python keyword-only separator syntax is not automatically part of ZR.
cases.zr_contract: ZR does not inherit Python's bare-star keyword-only separator unless the language surface defines it explicitly.
cases.expected_outcome: Python-specific keyword-only syntax is rejected explicitly
assertions: parser rejects the separator token pattern
diagnostic names the unsupported form
project compile path rejects the same surface

exception: Python-specific keyword-only syntax is rejected explicitly

binary.skip_reason: project/runtime suites already prove binary execution; this reference case keeps per-case executable parity disabled until the manifest runner can require executed_via=binary

cases.upstream_intent: Lua call sugar should not leak into ZR callable syntax.
cases.zr_contract: ZR callable syntax does not auto-adopt Lua's table or string call sugar.
cases.expected_outcome: Lua-only call sugar is rejected before lowering
assertions: parser emits a targeted unsupported-syntax diagnostic
compiler never lowers the form as a normal call
runtime tests remain unchanged because the call never compiles

exception: Lua-only call sugar is rejected before lowering
